classes.description: Creates a container that holds the modal dialog with proper padding and shadows.
Adds proper styling to the modal dialog’s body text.
Adds proper styling to the modal dialog’s header.
Adds the desired spacing to the row of button actions.
Adds styling for potentially dangerous actions.
Makes the container take up as much of the screen as possible.

attributes.description: Wires up the element to the modal controller. This may be a .s-modal element or a wrapper element.
Wires up the element that is to be shown/hidden
Designates which element to focus on modal show. If absent, defaults to the first focusable element within the modal.
Wires up the element that is to be shown/hidden
Wires up the element that is to be shown/hidden
Designates the element to return focus to when the modal is closed. If left unset, focus is not altered on close.
Removes the modal from the DOM entirely when it is hidden

events.description: Fires immediately before showing the modal. Calling .preventDefault() cancels the display of the modal.
Fires after the modal has been visually shown
Fires immediately before hiding the modal. Calling .preventDefault() cancels the removal of the modal.
Fires after the modal has been visually hidden

eventDetails.description: Contains the Element that initiated the event. For instance, the button clicked to show, the element clicked outside the modal that caused it to hide, etc.
Contains the Element to return focus to on hide. If a value is set to this property inside an event listener, it will be updated on the controller as well.

helpers.description: Helper to manually show an s-modal element via external JS
Helper to manually hide an s-modal element via external JS

accessibility.description: Supply the modal’s summary copy id. Assistive technologies (such as screen readers) use this to attribute to associate static text with a widget, element groups, headings, definitions, etc. (Source)

Informs assistive technologies (such as screen readers) if they should ignore the element. This should not be confused with the HTML5 hidden attribute which tells the browser to not display an element. (Source)
Labels the element for assistive technologies (such as screen readers). (Source)
Supply the modal’s title id here. Assistive technologies (such as screen readers) use this to attribute to catalog the document objects correctly. (Source)
Identifies dialog elements for assistive technologies (Source)
Helps assistive technologies to switch their reading mode from the larger document to a focused dialog window. (Source)
